description: MCP server letting LLMs author, inspect, measure and iterate on 3D CAD models with OpenCASCADE via the OCCTSwift family. 59 typed MCP tools (Swift, primary) + 37-tool Node subset, plus execute_script.
rules: OCCTMCP is an MCP server, not a library — clients call typed tools over stdio MCP. Two interchangeable servers read/write the same manifest.json scene: the Swift binary (occtmcp-server, 59 tools, primary) and the Node server (dist/index.js, 37-tool).

Prefer a typed tool over execute_script: boolean_op / transform_body / compute_metrics / measure_deviation / render_preview / generate_drawing etc. cover most flows. Drop to execute_script (arbitrary Swift on OCCTSwift) for geometry they can't make.

The scene is a manifest.json + per-body BREP files in the output directory; every tool resolves a body by its string `bodyId` from that manifest. execute_script (re)builds the scene; the other tools read or mutate it.
Tools take a single JSON object argument and return JSON text. Fallible inputs (missing bodyId, invalid path) come back as an error result, not a crash.
measure_distance is the MINIMUM gap (≈0 for overlapping bodies); use measure_deviation for one-sided / symmetric surface Hausdorff when certifying a reconstruction against a source mesh.
compute_metrics `boundingBox` is the default Bnd_Box (over-reports curved B-spline faces); request `boundingBoxOptimal` for a tight AddOptimal extent.

Swift-only tools (absent from Node): selection/remap (select_topology, remap_selection, find_correspondences, select_by_feature, list/clear_selections), annotations (add_dimension, add_scene_primitive, auto_dimension, show_bounding_box), reconstruct_*.

read_brep / import_file gate on shape validity by default; pass `allowInvalid: true` to load a loose-face / invalid in-progress reconstruction so compute_metrics / measure_deviation / validate_geometry can measure it.

import_file is the single entry point for STEP / IGES / OBJ / BREP (`.brep` / `.brp`, auto-detected by extension or `format: "brep"`). On Node, BREP routes to the occtkit load-brep verb (occtkit `import` lacks a BREP loader); on Swift it loads in-process.
